page: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, which provides 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached resources. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in compute-heavy and cryptographic tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the top 25% of tested servers. Passmark CPU and encryption tests also rank in the top 25%, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is in the top 25%, uncached large-block read and write operations fall into the middle 50%. In language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for text generation using a small 135M parameter model, but drops to average performance for larger models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c6a.12xlarge offers a dense compute profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it suitable for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff, as disk-intensive operations will be constrained by network-attached storage performance and the 19 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This instance is suited for batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong CPU and cryptographic performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, uncached memory bandwidth and larger model inference fall into average ranges. This profile makes the c6a.12xlarge suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, static web serving, and distributed analytics where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. It delivers strong CPU and cryptographic performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring forty-eight vCPUs and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for high-density processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory at a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers top 25% performance in stress-ng and Passmark CPU benchmarks
Provides 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics
